items.title: New Rule Amends No Surprises Act Dispute Process for Out-of-Network Health Care Payments - Jones Day
items.reason: This item directly addresses rulemaking that amends the No Surprises Act's Independent Dispute Resolution process, which is a core component of the federal law's implementation and directly impacts revenue-cycle operations.
items.summary: Jones Day reports on a new rule that amends the No Surprises Act's dispute resolution process for out-of-network health care payments. The rule modification affects how Independent Dispute Resolution (IDR) proceedings are conducted for balance-billing disputes between insurers and providers regarding out-of-network care costs. Revenue-cycle teams rely on the IDR process to resolve payment disputes and enforce balance-billing protections, making changes to its procedures directly relevant to claims management, payment collection, and compliance operations. The specific details of which provisions were amended, effective dates, and implementation requirements would be critical for revenue-cycle staff to understand operational impacts.
